title: Hydration first
bullets: Plain water is the default foundation during a fast.
If plain water feels flat, sparkling water (unflavored) is a common choice for people who tolerate it.

title: Electrolytes (carefully)
bullets: Some people add a pinch of salt to water if they feel lightheaded (not universal—stop if it doesn’t feel right).
Sweetened electrolyte drinks break a strict fast because of calories and often sugar.

title: Tea and coffee (choose your rules)
bullets: Many fasting styles allow plain tea or black coffee without milk or sugar.
If you’re unsure what counts as “still fasting” for your goals, pick the stricter line: water only.

title: When to pause
bullets: If you feel faint, confused, or unwell, end the fast and eat something gentle.
Don’t treat discomfort as a badge—safety comes first.

title: Gentle refeed (plant-forward basics)
bullets: Start with something small you tolerate well: broth, soup, toast, rice, fruit, or a modest snack.
Add a fuller meal later if you’re hungry—ease in instead of slamming calories all at once.

title: Gentle refeed (vegan)
bullets: Try miso soup, fruit, or a small grain bowl with vegetables.
Add protein (tofu, lentils) in the next meal if you want more staying power.

title: Gentle refeed (pescatarian)
bullets: Fish with mild seasoning can be easy to digest after a long fast for some people.
Pair it with cooked vegetables or rice for balance.

title: Gentle refeed (meat-forward)
bullets: Some people prefer a small portion of eggs or lean meat after fasting—others feel better with broth first.
Listen to your gut: volume matters as much as the food choice.

title: Watch the sugar spike
bullets: Huge doses of refined sugar right after fasting can feel rough for some people.
Fiber + protein tends to mellow the spike—when your diet allows it.

title: Vegetarian balance
bullets: Aim for protein across the window: beans, lentils, tofu, dairy/eggs (if vegetarian).
Add colorful vegetables for fiber and micronutrients.

title: Meat-forward balance
bullets: If meals skew animal protein, consider adding bone broth-derived minerals or vegetables if you tolerate them.
Variety across the week reduces “same meal every day” fatigue.

title: Omnivore template
bullets: Plate method: protein + carb + veg is a durable default.
Fish days and plant-forward days both fit—consistency beats labeling.

title: Snack strategy
bullets: Mini snacks beat going from “zero to feast” if your energy crashes mid-window.
Pair carbs with protein when you want steadier fullness.
